79061.name: Northeast Warehouse - Basement
79061.description: Walls of untidy brickwork hold back the earth in this underground room. a stairway leads up into a foul smelling warehouse, whose sturdy wooden floor provides a ceiling here. Besides the brick barriers which play a structural role, a further wall to your west seems, based on its less careful assembly, to exist purely for the concealment of another room.

79062.name: Northeast Warehouse - Basement
79062.description: Traces of smoke drift before you from the west.

79063.name: Northeast Warehouse - Basement
79063.description: Poonk smoke wafts thickly into the room from an opening to the south, causing your eyes to water a little. Walls of crude brick block your way to the north and west, and you can see from between the cracks that they hold back solid earth.

79064.name: Harbnar's Den
79064.description: Low, luxuriant cushions form a rough horseshoe around a central table here. The stench of stale smoke hangs in the air, and fresher wisps drift visibly around you. Expensive-looking rugs cover much of the cold stone floor, but these have turned dull with ash from countless poonk cigarettes.

79000.name: Illicit Port
79000.description: Nestled behind the imposing rock face to your north, this cove is invisible to any passing vessel not specifically seeking it out. All around you, rugged maritime subordinates busily heave unlawful cargo ashore and drag it up the beach.

79001.name: Top of the Beach
79001.description: Shingle and earth mingle here as beach gradually becomes road. The rough track ascending inland to your east is more the result of the regular pedestrian transit which takes place here than of any deliberate construction effort - there seems to be a constant flow of traffic between the cove to your west and some destination at the end of the track.

79002.name: Busy Coastal Path
79002.description: What was once lush meadow has been pounded into dry, red earth by the constant passage of feet here. The path you are standing on follows the natural slope between the expanse of pebble beach to your west and the verdent countryside around you. The cool, salty breeze from the west may not be appropriate for some flora, but tough, springy heather is eking out an existence without much trouble.

79003.name: Clifftop Meadow
79003.description: What a view! The immense expanse of water to your west is laid out before you like some titanic blanket of rich marine colour. As far as the eye can see, placid ocean rolls idly, an infinite sheet of turquoise and blue. Gulls soar above you, tossed playfully on the refreshing, salty breeze. Below you at the base of a steep cliff of earth, and sprawling southward, untouched virgin sands lie tranquil and perfect beside the sea.

79004.name: End of the Meadow
79004.description: As the cliff changes from soft earth to a more rocky composition, the coverage of bracken and grass tapers into total absence. To your south, a treacherous natural wall of rock makes further cliff travel from this angle impossible. The beach is accessible over the cliff, but it looks like a pretty high drop. It might be safer to head north, where pervasive bracken provides more comfortable footing.

79005.name: On the Beach
79005.description: Mud, earth and shale from the eroding clifftop above you mingle with the sand here to give this section of the beach a rather dirty composition, soiling your footwear and marring the natural beauty of the sands. To the north, shingle prevails over sand, and you can see a discrete cove which has obviously been converted into some form of covert landing point. To the south, the interference of humanity fades as the shore continues, sandwiched between cliff and ocean.

79006.name: Before a Shallow Cave
79006.description: The rock which makes up the cliff section here provides optimal conditions for the growth of barnacles. You can make out the threshold of the highest tides by the rough line at which the crustaceans cease to cling to the rocks. Whether by biological action or natural erosion, a shallow dip in the rock occurs here, and you could duck underneath the cliff a little if you chose to.

79007.name: Beside the Sea
79007.description: Damp mist swirling on the brisk air obscures your vision a little here, but you can still make out a fair bit of the ocean. Cold salt water splashes at your feet playfully, and they make deep impressions in the sand which quickly fill with water as tiny waves move back and forth in a ceaseless cycle of tidal movement.

79008.name: Within a Shallow Cave
79008.description: You duck underneath the natural shelf of rock here, being careful not to incur any painful scrapes on the formidable array of barnacles, mussels and other sharp sealife which clings tenaciously to the cliff. At high tide, the sea must submerge these animals, sustaining them with nutritious particles from the watery depths.

79009.name: End of the Beach
79009.description: It is impossible to walk further down the sands, as the way is blocked by sheer cliffs to the south, and the rolling waves to the west. A cursory glance reveals the rocks to your east to be impassable, but your view of the cliff itself is obscured by copious sea-grass and heather piling over the edge.

79011.name: On the Beach
79011.description: Debris kicked from the beach path directly to your north combined with clay and dirt fallen from the cliff above form a blanket of material to such a degree that they give the beach here a crunchy texture. Ascending the cliff here is wholly impossible, but a short distance north the summit is far closer to the sand, and it seems a path has been created to exploit this geography.

79012.name: South of the Estuary
79012.description: Fresh water gushes through the estuary into the sea to the north, as if almost eager to escape the rotting jungle. The steep rise of a cliff path to the south divides the disgusting foliage from the beautiful ocean.

79013.name: Rough Cliff Path
79013.description: Consistent pedestrian travel along this section of cliff has wrought a barren, dusty channel through the grass. Protruding plant roots and stones have been worn to a gem-like sheen by passing feet, though they still impinge on your progress, causing you to trip and stumble with some regularity.

79014.name: Rough Cliff Path
79014.description: The biting marine winds are a lot more noticeable up here. Thin stalks of grey- green grass bend hard under the gusts rolling up the cliffs which numb the exposed parts of your body. The clifftop path peaks to the south, and a partially toppled wall of stacked slate rises to waist level, grass spewing from its worn interior.

79015.name: Rough Cliff Path
79015.description: The raging ocean wind generates a backdrop of noise, a constant dull roar assailing your ears as you soldier along the clifftops. A low stone wall to your east runs parallel to the coastal precipice, restricting your travel to either the north or the south - the direction of the cliff path.

79016.name: Dusty Clearing
79016.description: The surrounding fertile ground has here been rendered bare, presumably via regular footfall. A stout wooden stile permits access into a wide field to the east while preventing animals from leaving - perhaps this area once had some agrarian purpose.

79017.name: Clifftop Meadow
79017.description: A vast, verdant field, presumably abandoned, stretches over the cliffs. The derelict pasture is contained within a series of fishbone slate barriers, tumbled in some places, and slowly receding into the surroundings under the influence of nature. A wooden stile would once have controlled the movements of the livestock living here, though now seems redundant as any residents of this meadow have since departed.

79018.name: Steep Coastal Incline
79018.description: The soil on this sharp beachward grade is saturated with clay, preventing the growth of sea-grasses and other flora. Without the stabilising presence of plant roots, this section of cliff seems to be collapsing towards the ocean, providing a viable route to the sands. To both the west and east however, the lower concentration of clay is such that the cliffs remain fully intact.

79019.name: Sheltered Bay
79019.description: Protected from tempestuous coastal gales by a bold spit of land rising directly to the north, the water here lies placidly flat. It seems somebody has taken advantage of this small natural harbour, as you spy an array of small- scale shipping beached to the east, apparently still in use.

79020.name: Spit of Sand
79020.description: The chance topology of the littoral here is such that this area is covered by the tides at a slower rate than the surrounding sand, forming a small, dry peninsula of beach, surrounded by shallow water on all sides but the east.

79021.name: Atop a Small Dune
79021.description: The sound of the lapping tides indicates your proximity to the shoreline. The adjacent sections of sand are partially covered by the shallow waters, but the presence of a small, naturally raised dune here, means your feet stay dry. The bank extends into the sea to the west.

79022.name: Water's Edge
79022.description: The sand here at the very end of the beach is perfectly flattened, and made darker too by the exogenesis of miniature waves. The action of the sea has generated a cool, refreshing bank of vaporous mist.

79023.name: Near the Coast
79023.description: All around you, the tranquil waters of the Chardaleth Sea lap in relative calm. To the west the intensity of the waves increases, the ocean lacking the protection of landmasses. Conversely, a magnificent coastline rises in the opposite direction, clean beaches developing into high, fertile cliffs.

79024.name: Near the Coast
79024.description: The water lies undisturbed, a perfect sheet of blue and grey, immune here to the agitation of the wind. Banks of low mist roll around you and on towards the shining wet sands of the beach to your east.

79025.name: The Open Sea
79025.description: Floating about on the Chardaleth Sea, you can make out little in any direction for the thick fog which surrounds you. The faint outline of a shore to the east is barely visible, but other than that you can distinguish nothing but the grey water below you and the wall of white around you.

79026.name: Near the Coast
79026.description: A sliver of wet beach can be seen to the east, wedged between the ocean and the cliff. The water here is very shallow, and on the sandy surface beneath you, evidence of various primitive sea-life is apparent.

79027.name: Near the Coast
79027.description: A raised bank of sand underneath you causes a slight irregularity in the current here, and the lesser distance from the surface accomodates a certain relative warmth in the water. You can almost touch the bottom here, and the water gets shallower still as the sandbank rises towards the beach to the east.

79028.name: Busy Coastal Path
79028.description: Here the path weaves between a large outcrop of rock to the south and a derelict agricultural barrier of slate to the north. Wind blowing in from the sea to the west funnels through this natural bottleneck, chilling you a little.

79029.name: Busy Coastal Path
79029.description: The butts of many a spent poonk cigarette litter the grassy boundaries of this well-trodden footpath. Most lie mummified by the dust, though some seem fresh, suggesting recent activity.

79030.name: Busy Coastal Path
79030.description: This path traces the southern boundary of a long abandoned pasture here. Though for the most part the field is inaccesible, the wall directly to your north is damaged to such an extent that you could quite easily wander over its remains.

n.to_room_name: Among the Ruins of the Wall

79031.name: Busy Coastal Path
79031.description: The aural backdrop of the coastal moors which you expect - the cackle of gulls, the ceaseless roar of the wind - is replaced here by somewhat less natural cacophony. From the south and east, the sounds of heated conversation and the periodic clunking of metal and wood punctuate some unseen activity. Whatever shenanigans are underway, however, are rendered invisible to you, your view to the south being blocked by a natural shelf of rock.

n.to_room_name: Among the Ruins of the Wall

79032.name: Before a Suspicious Shack
79032.description: In stark contrast to the ancient network of stone walls that snake around the fields in this area, the tall wooden structure immediately to your north seems to have been freshly constructed. What purpose it could serve escapes you, but its rugged and plain appearance suggests functionality rather than aesthetics.

79033.name: Outside Port Larceny
79033.description: Nestled behind a tall natural hill of grey granite separating them from the coast, an array of large wooden buildings lies before you within a shallow, verdant valley. This can be none other than the infamous smuggling company of Fidel McGee.

79034.name: Port Larceny
79034.description: The hills are alive, with the sound of... smuggling. Among the rolling acclivities of the southwestern countryside, a band of sea-faring crooks have established their own trafficking haven. Loud negotiation in a variety of languages drifts to your ears from within an arrangement of warehouses and huts, supplemented with a constant chorus of grunting, cursing and the thudding and grating of heavy objects being moved.

79035.name: Clifftop Meadow
79035.description: The intersection of two haphazard walls marks the northwestern corner of this meadow. Over the wall to the west, you spy the ocean not a long way off, but the wall is impassible. Clumps of unmaintained grass burst from the fertile ground beneath you, and in all directions not walled off.

79036.name: Clifftop Meadow
79036.description: A tall but fast deteriorating fishbone wall of dusty slate seperates this abandoned meadow from the rampant foliage of the western Talrynian swamps. The feral utterances of exotic jungle animals and birds drift faintly from the bottom of the hill beyond the barricade, but here atop the cliffs, all is peaceful.

79037.name: Clifftop Meadow
79037.description: Playfully rolling on sporadic thermals and gusts, the presence of a seabird entourage in the skies some distance to your west suggests your relative proximity to the coast. You soak in the clean, refreshing air, the soft, healthy grasses compressed gently beneath your feet.

79038.name: Clifftop Meadow
79038.description: A fair distance from the sea, this part of the field is less prone to the brisk winds which rise to the clifftops from the water than the more westerly sections of agrarian land. Benign coastal fauna enjoy the relative placidity. A number of wild ponies graze lazily, and delicate insects travel from plant to plant, exploring their miniature world entirely oblivious to your presence.

79039.name: Clifftop Meadow
79039.description: An abundance of delicate purple flowers litter the carpet of turf which comprises this pleasant meadow atop the cliffs. The avian denizens of the Chardaleth and its eastern coastline maintain a cacophonous chorus above you in the clear firmament, delicate wings traversing the azure empyrean with prowess and grace.

79040.name: Beside the Wall
79040.description: Though thoroughly worn by the elements to the point of near destruction, the resilience of this low slate blockade which segments the cliffs is remarkable - in this section at least, it is still standing, and prevents you from crossing over to the trail which you see over it to the south. Surveying the rest of the wall's length however, you see that the same cannot be said, and in several places in the distance you notice much more substantial collapse.

79041.name: Beside the Wall
79041.description: No real means by which to gauge the true age of the barrier which rises before you exist, but you can guess at centuries. During this time, generation after generation of fertile grasses, under the natural guidance of Isneen, have infiltrated the cracks in the wall, slowly absorbing it back into the countryside. However, such is the meticulously careful nature of its construction that here at least, it still stands tall enough to block your way.

e.to_room_name: Among the Ruins of the Wall

79042.name: Among the Ruins of the Wall
79042.description: A boundary once dilligently erected by some ancestral proprieter of livestock has since crumbled to such an extent that you now stand amidst chunks of it, explosive clumps of cliff-grass hungrily enveloping its remains. The exhaustive manual toil of the wall's creator is still evident in the neat alignment of the many slate fragments comprising its shattered form. It seems a pity that such hard work should, through prolonged neglect, amount to this mess of fallen stone.

e.to_room_name: Among the Ruins of the Wall

79043.name: Clifftop Meadow
79043.description: Turf bursts zealously from the soft earth all around you. It is easy to see why this area would once have made brilliant farmland, the healthy grass is testament to the soil's powerful fertility. For decades, generations of ex- domestic animals have grazed the field unchecked, yet still the grasses spring vigorously forth.

s.to_room_name: Among the Ruins of the Wall

79044.name: Clifftop Meadow
79044.description: The humidity of the swamp to the north can be felt here despite the stiff wall of stones which holds it back. It is as if the jungle were sentient and crawling deliberately towards this idyll of a meadow like a puma stalking a fawn. The stench of the forest hangs in the air, but so far the pasture remains unsullied.

79045.name: Clifftop Meadow
79045.description: Two walls meet at a right-angle here, marking the northwesterly corner of the abandoned meadow. The barrier to your north stands tall, protecting the carpet of grass and flowers from the destructive influence of the Talrynian Forest's grasping foliage. The barrier to the east is broken down in places, some areas low enough that you could easily cross over into the neighboring countryside.

79046.name: Clifftop Meadow
79046.description: Bulky shards of slate stretch north to south in a resilient herringbone pattern which seems to have stood the test of time. The wall marks the eastern boundary of the meadow. Beyond it, fields, moorland and later prairie lie beneath the shelf of the sky, grey clouds sailing above through patches of clear air.

s.to_room_name: Among the Ruins of the Wall

79047.name: Among the Ruins of the Wall
79047.description: The natural decay of countless years has been partly responsible for the decline of this ancient stone barrier, but much of the debris appears to have been manipulated and cleared more recently by human effort. Immediately to your east a wooden hut has been constructed, and stones from the wall have been pushed westward to make room for its creation.

w.to_room_name: Among the Ruins of the Wall

79048.name: Port Larceny
79048.description: Tall sheds rise all around you, their wooden frames fresh and so far not tainted by the degrading influence of the elements, suggesting fairly recent construction. The wide doors of the structures to both your east and west are wide open, and an eruption of activity can be heard, and partially witnessed, within.

79049.name: Northwest Warehouse
79049.description: Piled to the ceiling, robust wooden crates are constantly added to and taken from by an army of grimy smugglers. Gazing upward past the towering summits of the crate-stacks all around, you notice that a series of walkways amidst the rafters of this building create a first floor of sorts, allowing both for easier manipulation of the goods stored here and for more storage overall.

79050.name: Northwest Warehouse
79050.description: Not much natural illumination enters through the small gaps in the simple wooden walls of this gargantuan smuggling cache, but the huge room is sufficiently lit by way of a series of classic Mareldjan light-emitting globes, presumably filched from the streets of the Crown on the Water itself.

79051.name: Northwest Warehouse
79051.description: A hybrid step and ladder arrangement here leads upward towards a haphazard first floor, where further loot lies, in a similar fashion to the plunder around you, concealed in broad crates of resilient wood. Wondering for a moment how such huge boxes could be carried up such a flimsy pseudo-staircase, you glance up and realise that a complex system of ropes and pulleys adorn the ceiling, enabling the vertical movement of smuggled goods.

79052.name: Northwest Warehouse
79052.description: Unlike that of the rest of the complex, the floor here is cold, packed earth. Clearly those in charge of preparing the warehouse were unable to complete the decking which you see around its other parts before it was put into use. Crates of contraband are piled here despite this, though in somewhat lower quantity due to the unevenness of the ground.

79057.name: Northeast Warehouse
79057.description: It is immediately obvious what cargo is kept in this vast shed, the smell is overpowering and unmistakable. Enough poonk to satisfy the whole poonk-smoking continent for a good few months is stashed inside the crates and sacks you see all around you, some actually warping for being packed so tightly.

79058.name: Northeast Warehouse
79058.description: The walls of the warehouse seem to sway a little as you involuntarily inhale the pervasive fumes emanating from a huge pile of sacks in the corner here. The sacked poonk unleashes a much more powerful aroma than its boxed counterpart.

79059.name: Northeast Warehouse
79059.description: The air is thick with the potent stench of bootlegged poonk. The walls of the warehouse rise to the west and south, blocking it in and preventing fresh air from diluting the smell at all. The concentration is so severe that the fumes alone are beginning to adversely affect you.

79060.name: Northeast... Warehouse?
79060.description: Feeling faint, you survey your surroundings as best you can, breathing lightly so as to avoid the stifling scent of stockpiled poonk. It is hard to find your bearings, but it seems a series of steps have been dug into the floor of the warehouse here, leading into an underground section.

79065.name: Port Larceny
79065.description: A dusty track slices through the country from north to south, flanked by massive sheds on either side. Small cultures of grass at the bases of the buildings make a final stand against the ceaseless onslaught of feet, their central cousins worn into utter oblivion through excessive pedestrian activity.

79066.name: Port Larceny
79066.description: Two paths intersect here, forming a crossroads between the four large warehouses of the Port. The ground is compacted and utterly dry, punished by the soles of smuggler footwear to a far greater extent than the rest of the Port.

79067.name: Port Larceny
79067.description: The southern road of the Port leads away from the central crossroads and downhill between two buildings. The structure to your east is unremarkable - a simple shed of low quality wood - but the western building has clearly been more carefully constructed, with clean, even brickwork comprising the lower parts of the wall, and tidy beams of stained timber rising to connect it with the roof.

79068.name: Port Larceny
79068.description: You stand at the southernmost point of Port Larceny. Before you to the south, open coastal countryside rolls into the distance for many leagues. Beaches, fields and cliffs are rendered miniature in your vision by the natural laws of perspective. The buildings which flank you on either side to the east and west both have doors here.

79069.name: Port Larceny
79069.description: The ground here sustains a partial covering of springy coastal grass, perhaps due to a lack of pedestrianism. The walls of warehouses stretch high to your north and south, blocking a lot of natural light.

79070.name: Port Larceny
79070.description: A low, posh-looking building lies immediately to your west, presenting a simple door with no obvious handle or knob. The patch of ground which supports you is otherwise sandwiched between a pair of tall sheds to the north and south, the former plain and unremarkable, the latter presenting more careful architecture.

79071.name: Port Larceny
79071.description: The ground here must be rich in some natural fertiliser, as thin grasses spring up with remarkable vitality quite suddenly. A stench hangs in the air, wafting from the east and causing your nose to wrinkle involuntarily. The unpleasant aroma emanates from between the gaps in a rotten shack immediately to your east.

79072.name: The Loo
79072.description: A crude lavatory permits waste into the ground in the centre of this toilet hut, a small barrel opening into a vertical tunnel which presumably terminates somewhere near the ocean. The overpowering reek all around you would suggest that the journey isn't always fully completed though. McGee's employees must be pretty rough and ready in order to grin and bear this dreadful room. Any port in a storm, I guess.

79073.name: Southeast Warehouse
79073.description: The products being manipulated in this warehouse clank as the smugglers heave them around, and judging by the cumbersome nature of the crates, the plunder has a lot of weight. Hung on the wall, for display rather than storage, an array of shields, armor and other military hardware can be seen.

79074.name: Southeast Warehouse
79074.description: A plethora of voulges, halberds and other exotic polearms obscures the northern wall - enough weaponry to equip an formidable army several times over. Dotted around the warehouse, full sets of armor are carefully arranged on wooden frames, and interestingly not all of them seem humanoid in design.

79075.name: Southeast Warehouse
79075.description: Laid out with military precision and discipline in a series of low cases, a selection of blades in many shapes and sizes shimmers impressively before your eyes. The wares here have been polished and sharpened with obsessive dilligence in order to entice prospective customers.

79076.name: Southeast Warehouse
79076.description: Crates and boxes have been pushed aside here, leaving a wide space of open wooden floor. A simple table lies in the middle of the room, and a wooden screen seperates the area from the section of warehouse to your west.

79077.name: Port Larceny Headquarters
79077.description: Sustained and profitable wheeling and dealing has enabled the upper echelons of the Port's chain of command to really pull out all the stops with the interior of this particular building. The floor of this foyer is neatly tiled, and the ornamental decoration of the walls and surfaces exudes affluence while avoiding gaudyness.

79078.name: Port Larceny Headquarters
79078.description: The architect of this complex had a good sense of common look and feel - the decor is tasteful and utterly uniform. Your feet echo on the carefully tiled floor, the sound reverberating through the open passageways to the north and to the east.

79079.name: Port Larceny Headquarters
79079.description: The focal point of this room is a heavy door in the eastern wall, clearly built to resist the most egregious physical punishment. Rays of light emitted by a shining globe mounted in the ceiling bounce off the plates and bands of unyielding metal just as any attempted battery of the door surely would.

79080.name: Bottom of the Stairs
79080.description: A slim staircase of polished wood glides up the eastern wall of this room, carpeted in rich burgundy. Paintings of grody, unsavoury looking characters grin down at you from their frames, hung alongside the stairs in an ascending row.

79081.name: Cluttered Office
79081.description: Several polished tables are dotted around the room, each piled high with scrolls of paperwork. This is where the numbers are crunched, the losses and profits calculated, and the plunder equitably divided. One would never expect such a level of professionalism from such notorious criminals, but the careful bookkeeping and record taking you witness here is testament to the shrewdness of McGee's smugglers.

79087.name: Granite Headland
79087.description: Erosion by the eternal battery of the Chardaleth Sea has occured to the north, leaving nothing but a gaping chasm and receding beach of shingle far below. However, the igneous rock of this headland has resisted the water well, leaving the batholith on which you stand jutting proudly from the coast.

79088.name: Granite Headland
79088.description: Flecks of black, white and grey are embedded in the rock beneath your hands and feet. From atop this tor you can make out a long sandy beach to the south, engulfed by ocean mists at its southernmost end. To the east, the green meadowland of the southern countryside spans into the distance, and to the north the deep blue water of Baranath Bay undulates vigourously.

79129.name: Within a Suspicious Shack
79129.description: Inside this building you can scarcely move a muscle for all the luggage compactly heaped before you. Face flat against the wall of boxes and sacks, you catch a strong whiff of sea salt. The mountain of goods that fill this hut must recently have spent a while at sea.

79800.name: Rolling Meadow
79800.description: The tall grasses of the field around you grow well past your waist, untouched by the grazing animals of the western meadow. The drop down to the swamp to the north is steep enough to make you dizzy as you peer over the edge. Humidity and the acrid smell of swamp combined with the swarming gnats that swarm near your face compel you to hasten your pace.

79801.name: Rolling Meadow
79801.description: The grass here is mostly green and healthy, but in some spots worms have begun their insidious work, turning large patches of the field a diseased brown. As you trip over a particularly thick bramble of tall grass, you notice a small stone marker set into the mud. It could be your imagination, but an indentation in the mud looks suspiciously like a human footprint.

79802.name: Rolling Meadow
79802.description: You may as well stand at the nexus of the world, for the diverse scenery around you puts you as much at ease as it takes your breath away. The meadow plateau ends at the cliffs to the east, and from here you can see the low dusty gray outline of the city of Oldgate far across the swamp to the north. To the south, the field changes abruptly into thick woods that grow up the slope of the southern mountain.

79803.name: By the Brook
79803.description: The stone wall that divides this meadow from the next ends abruptly where a brook leading down from the southern mountain ends in a small pond. Beyond it, a line of trees marks the edge of the forest that covers the base of the mountain. Following the brook south and to the east, the quiet desolation of the meadow and the forest begins to press on your mind.

79804.name: Rolling Meadow
79804.description: Bees and tiny elusive gnats hover busily over the white and yellow daisies that dapple the green of the meadow in all directions. The tall grasses becomes more sparse as they break over the more rocky ground near the tree line to the south. As you wander contentedly, you begin to take notice of strange disturbances in the grasses, as if someone had repeatedly forced their way through yet made some attempt to cover their tracks.

79805.name: A Cliff-Top Forest
79805.description: The trees here grow thick and strong, fallen branches littering the ground making movement difficult. The foliage is so thick that passage to the south is completely blocked, and the trees near the cliff are thankfully too thick to break through. Never-the-less, you take pains to ensure that you don't wander too close to the edge.

79806.name: A Suspicious Copse
79806.description: The grove is dark and close, the only sound that breaks through the silence of the forest being the brook that meanders around to the south and west. Looking around, you only narrowly miss tripping over a thin rope spread low between two trees. Tracing the line, you encounter a chain of cheaply made metal chimes hidden amongst the grass. Gazing intently around, you notice that the foliage to the east appears to be of a different color than the rest.

79807.name: A Concealed Campsite
79807.description: Branches have been pulled down from trees and arranged to conceal what upon closer inspection is clearly a camp of some sort. Behind the small covered embers of a campfire is a canvas tent.

79010.name: Amidst the Heather
79010.description: Clinging to the ample plant life around you, you find yourself able to traverse the low-quality staircase beneath your feet with relative ease. As you grope for handholds in the bracken, the occasional sharp sprig of gorse or abrasive rock causes you to wince, but for the most part the climb is painless and straight-forward.

79053.name: Northwest Warehouse - First Floor
79053.description: A suspended platform of boards clings to the western wall of the warehouse, and it is upon this rather rickety construction which you now stand. A sturdy, well-made door, contrasting the spontaneous, hasty and low quality architecture of the rest of the building leads west, with a single square window set into it at face level.

79054.name: Northwest Warehouse - First Floor
79054.description: A spell of vertigo hits you for a moment - the boardwalk on which you stand has no obvious barrier preventing those who travese it from plummiting to the warehouse floor should they stray a little too far to the east, a prospect none too appealing as it lies some distance below you. A simple, poorly constructed door lies in the western wall, while the platform comprising the second floor of the warehouse runs southward.

79055.name: Storage Cupboard
79055.description: In a building whose sole purpose is storage, it strikes you as a little odd that such a room should exist. Nonetheless, this dark room is home to an abundance of shelves, though rather than the boxed plunder of the warehouse outside, they instead hold, among other things, mountains of paperwork, scroll after scroll of figures pertaining to the aforementioned booty.

79056.name: Spink's Office
79056.description: The decor of this room immediately implies that the occupant is a wizard of some sort. A glass display box mounted on the wall showcases a variety of exotic wands, and a broad spectrum of scrolls and arcane literature are stored neatly along varnished wooden shelves. A desk, covered in financial documents, lies in the center of the room.

79082.name: Carpeted Landing
79082.description: The deep red of the carpet here is unsullied by age, suggesting either that it has been laid recently or has been consistently well cared for. Ornate patterns adorn the walls, representations of vines snaking across the surfaces.

79083.name: Grift's Office
79083.description: The first thing you notice as you wander into this spacious office is the desk - it dominates - a formidable wooden block of absurd size which spans almost the width of the room. Paper is stacked neatly along one edge, and a long quill dripping with ink rests in a metal beaker alongside the documents. Stale poonk smoke hangs in the air like a smelly spectre.

79084.name: Treasury
79084.description: A series of iron chests line the northern wall of this dark, windowless room, secured to the floorboards with heavy bolts. Security has been the only goal in the design of this room, and the heavy padlocked eastern door, as well as the arrangement of the plunder-filled cases, testifies to this.

79085.name: Fidel's Office
79085.description: An immense pane of glass fills the southern wall, filling the room with light and allowing a marvellous view of the southern west coast. Inland, hills ascend into moorland, and on the coast a series of deserted beaches and rocky cliffs continue as far as the eye can see. Such is the grandeur of this elevated panorama that the details of the unremarkable office pale in comparison. McGee's desk is clean and empty, and the carpeted floor is unpatterned. The only noticable anomaly is a wide, low basket in the northwest corner - perhaps the bed of a canine companion to the famous smuggler.

79150.name: Fidel's Apartment
79150.description: A rough mat covers the floor, the dried clumps and streaks of dirt adorning it suggesting that it has been laid here for the wiping of unclean feet upon entry. Varnished panels of dark wood cover the walls, simple yet visually appealing.

79808.name: Inside the Tent
79808.description: Living must be rough for the occupants of this claustrophobic tent. Two sleeping rolls have been laid near the walls, and empty packets of rations and wrappers from meat pies litter the ground. A fairly detailed map of a large warehouse has been nailed to the main support beam in the center of the tent, the humidity making the parchment curl at the edges. The smell of filth is overwhelming and you struggle to breathe through your mouth to avoid the stench.
